label: Drinking water - groundwater
description: This indicator identifies the availability and quality of drinking water from groundwater sources for people within the Midwest Landscape. It prioritizes areas based on proximity to regionally extensive aquifer systems that have the potential to be used as a drinking water source. Due to the low resolution of the input data, we refine priority areas in this indicator by emphasizing the value of natural landcover types and protected lands, designated as natural assets in the legend. This indicator originates from the USGS Principal Aquifers of the United States and the USGS Alluvial and Glacial Aquifers layer.

values.label: Principal, alluvial, or glacial aquifer, not a natural asset
Principal, alluvial, or glacial aquifer, natural asset

label: Drinking water - surface water
description: This indicator identifies the availability and quality of drinking water from surface sources for people within the Midwest Landscape. It prioritizes areas based on supply and demand of drinking water, plus the presence of threats to the water supply. Due to the low resolution of the input data, we refine priority areas in this indicator by emphasizing the value of natural landcover types and protected lands, designated as natural assets in the legend. This indicator originates from the U.S. Forest Service National Forests to Faucets 2.0 Assessment.

values.label: Not identified as a priority area for surface drinking water
Less important watershed, not a natural asset
Medium important watershed, not a natural asset
More important watershed, not a natural asset

label: Hazard and disaster protection - natural hazards
description: This indicator represents the protection of communities vulnerable to impacts of hazards and other community-level stressors, which can include natural disasters (e.g. flooding, wildfire, etc.) or human-caused pollution. It prioritizes areas based on the predicted annual economic loss from riverine and coastal flooding, drought, and wildfires. Due to the low resolution of the input data, we refine priority areas in this indicator by emphasizing the value of natural landcover types and protected lands, designated as natural assets in the legend. This indicator originates from the FEMA Coastal Flooding, Drought, Riverine Flooding, and Wildfire data layers.

values.label: Lower risk of loss, not a natural asset
Lower risk of loss, natural asset
Medium risk of loss, not a natural asset
Medium risk of loss, natural asset
High risk of loss, not a natural asset
High risk of loss, natural asset

valueLabel: Expected annual economic loss from flooding, drought, and wildfires
captionLabel: hazard and disaster protection - natural hazards

label: Hazard and disaster protection - pollution
description: This indicator represents the protection of communities vulnerable to impacts of hazards and other community-level stressors, which can include natural disasters (e.g. flooding, wildfire, etc.) or human-caused pollution. It prioritizes areas based on the presence and amount of air and water pollutants. Due to the low resolution of the input data, we refine priority areas in this indicator by emphasizing the value of natural landcover types and protected lands, designated as natural assets in the legend. This indicator originates from the EPA Cumulative Respiratory Risk from Air Toxins and EPA Total Permitted Discharges.

values.label: Lower risk of air or water pollution, not a natural asset
Lower risk of air or water pollution, natural asset
Medium risk of air or water pollution, not a natural asset
Medium risk of air or water pollution, natural asset
Higher risk of air or water pollution, not a natural asset
Higher risk of air or water pollution, natural asset

label: Hazard and disaster protection - social vulnerability index
description: This indicator represents the protection of communities vulnerable to impacts of hazards and other community-level stressors, which can include natural disasters (e.g. flooding, wildfire, etc.) or human-caused pollution. It prioritizes areas of social vulnerability based on environmental and socioeconomic burdens. Due to the low resolution of the input data, we refine priority areas in this indicator by emphasizing the value of natural landcover types and protected lands, designated as natural assets in the legend. This indicator originates from the CDC Social Vulnerability Index.

values.label: Developed landcover (not assessed) or not identified as a vulnerable census tract
Moderately vulnerable census tract, not a natural asset
Moderately vulnerable census tract, natural asset
Highly vulnerable census tract, not a natural asset
Highly vulnerable census tract, natural asset

valueLabel: Relative vulnerability of census tracts to emergencies based on social factors
captionLabel: hazard and disaster protection - social vulnerability index

label: Hazard and disaster protection - tree canopy
description: This indicator represents the protection of communities vulnerable to impacts of hazards and other community-level stressors, which can include natural disasters (e.g. flooding, wildfire, etc.) or human-caused pollution. It prioritizes areas based on tree canopy cover need in populated areas based on existing tree canopy, surface temperature, socioeconomic factors, and health factors. Due to the low resolution of the input data, we refine priority areas in this indicator by emphasizing the value of natural landcover types and protected lands, designated as natural assets in the legend. This indicator originates from American Forests Tree Equity Score.

values.label: Not identified as a priority area for tree canopy cover
Moderate tree canopy need, not a natural asset
Moderate tree canopy need, natural asset
High tree canopy need, not a natural asset
High tree canopy need, natural asset
Highest tree canopy need, not a natural asset
Highest tree canopy need, natural asset

valueLabel: Tree canopy need in populated areas
captionLabel: hazard and disaster protection - tree canopy

label: Local economic vitality - grazing
description: This indicator identifies the economic support of local economies from natural resources or associated activities (e.g. grazing, timber, pollination, outdoor recreation, and fisheries). It prioritizes the value of grazing areas based on the five-year average pasture rental rate of Midwest counties. This indicator originates from the U.S. Census Bureau USA County Boundaries, USDA National Agricultural Statistics Service, and the Midwest Conservation Blueprint Grasslands Indicator.

values.label: Not identified as a priority area for grazing

valueLabel: 5-year average pasture rental rate, by county

label: Local economic vitality - pollination
description: This indicator identifies the economic support of local economies from natural resources or associated activities (e.g. grazing, timber, pollination, outdoor recreation, and fisheries). It prioritizes areas based on the presence of pollinator habitat and the economic value of insect pollination on agricultural crops within Midwest counties. This indicator originates from the U.S. Census Bureau USA County Boundaries, Jordan et al. 2021, Koh et al. 2016, and the USDA Cropland Data Layer.

values.label: Not identified as a priority area for agricultural pollination

valueLabel: Economic value of insect pollination on agricultural crops, by county

label: Local economic vitality - timber
description: This indicator identifies the economic support of local economies from natural resources or associated activities (e.g. grazing, timber, pollination, outdoor recreation, and fisheries). It prioritizes areas based on the total value economic value of timber returns within each Midwest county. This indicator originates from the U.S. Census Bureau USA County Boundaries, Cavender-Bares et al. 2022, the National Land Cover Database, and the Protected Areas Database.

values.label: Not identified as a priority area for timber harvest

valueLabel: Economic value of timber, by county

label: Nutrient reduction
description: This indicator represents the minimization of nutrient loading that leads to issues in downstream waters (e.g. harmful and nuisance algal blooms) in both the Gulf and the Great Lakes. It prioritizes areas based on a catchment's contribution to the loading of nitrogen, phosphorus, and suspended sediments into water. Due to the low resolution of the input data, we refine priority areas in this indicator by emphasizing the value of natural landcover types and protected lands, designated as natural assets in the legend. This indicator originates from the USGS 2012 SPARROW Model for the Midwest.

values.label: Developed landcover (not assessed) or no data available
Low nutrient load, not a natural asset
Medium nutrient load, not a natural asset
High nutrient load, not a natural asset

valueLabel: Combined nitrogen, phosphorus, and suspended sediment load

label: Potential access to recreation
description: This indicator identifies accessibility gaps to green space and outdoor recreation in populated areas within the Midwest Landscape. It prioritizes areas currently lacking access to parks within a 10-minute walk based on population density, community health, pollution burden, and more. Due to the low resolution of the input data, we refine priority areas in this indicator by emphasizing the value of natural landcover types and protected lands, designated as natural assets in the legend. This indicator originates from the Trust for Public Land's ParkServe dataset.

values.label: Not identified as a priority area for new parks
Lower priority, not a natural asset
Medium priority, not a natural asset
High priority, not a natural asset

valueLabel: Priority for a new park to serve people who lack nearby park access

label: Public recreation access
description: This indicator represents the ability to enjoy outdoor spaces (e.g. green spaces, wilderness areas, beaches/waters) for recreation and health purposes within the Midwest Landscape. It prioritizes areas based on locations of current public recreation sites. Due to the low resolution of the input data, we refine priority areas in this indicator by emphasizing the value of natural landcover types and protected lands, designated as natural assets in the legend. This indicator originates from the Protected Areas Database and Open Street Map roads.

values.label: Not identified as a public recreation area
Public recreation area, not a natural asset

label: Sustainable, working lands
description: This indicator represents the ability of land to economically support human communities while also providing conservation benefits within the Midwest Landscape. It prioritizes areas based on the locations of conservation easements. This indicator originates from the National Conservation Easement Database.

values.label: Not identified as a closed access or unknown access conservation easement
Closed access or unknown access conservation easement

label: Tribal conservation sovereignty
description: This indicator represents the ability of Tribal nations to have access to, and control of, their natural and cultural resources. It prioritizes areas based on the locations of Tribal Nations trust and fee lands. Due to the low resolution of the input data, we refine priority areas in this indicator by emphasizing the value of natural landcover types and protected lands, designated as natural assets in the legend. This indicator originates from the Bureau of Indian Affairs Land Area Representations.

values.label: Not identified as a Tribal trust or fee land
Tribal trust or fee land, not a natural asset
Tribal trust or fee land, natural asset

label: Aquatic network connectivity
description: This indicator identifies the ability of aquatic species to access large, complex aquatic networks. It prioritizes areas based on the length of hydrologically connected flowlines and diversity of unfragmented aquatic habitat in the region. This indicator originates from the Nature Conservancy's Resilient Rivers dataset and the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service's National Wetlands Inventory.

values.label: Not identified as an aquatic area

label: Climate resiliency
description: This indicator identifies the ability of lands and waters to function under changing climate conditions. It prioritizes areas based on landscape diversity and connectedness. This indicator originates from the Nature Conservancy's Resilient Land Mapping tool.

values.label: Developed landcover or aquatic area (not assessed)

label: Climate resiliency - carbon sequestration
description: This indicator identifies the ability of lands and waters to function under changing climate conditions. It prioritizes areas based on the amount of above and belowground carbon stored. Due to the low resolution of the input data, we refine priority areas in this indicator by emphasizing the value of natural landcover types and protected lands, designated as natural assets in the legend. This indicator originates from the U.N. Environment Programme World Biomass and Soil Carbon data.

values.label: Developed landcover or aquatic area (not assessed)
Lower carbon sequestration, not a natural asset
Medium carbon sequestration, not a natural asset
High carbon sequestration, not a natural asset

label: Forests
description: This indicator represents forest heath based on the availability and risk of mortality of forested stands within the Midwest Landscape. It prioritizes areas based on locations of forested stands and risks from major forest insects and diseases. This indicator originates from the U.S. Forest Service National Insect and Disease Risk Map and the National Land Cover Database.

values.label: Pixels at hazard for mortality losses exceeding 25% of their 2012 basal area over the 2013-2027 time frame
Pixels considered at risk in the National Insect and Disease 2012 assessment, but that are likely no longer at risk
Treed areas, but not modeled as "At Risk"

label: Grasslands
description: This indicator represents grassland health within the Midwest Landscape. It prioritizes areas based on the percentage of perennial grasses and forbs within an area. This indicator originates from the Rangeland Analysis Platform cover data.

label: Great Lakes shoreline & dune habitat
description: This indicator represents shoreline and dune health based on the naturalness of shoreline features and dune habitat. It prioritizes areas based on shoreline type (natural or artificial), landcover type (Great Lakes Dune or Great Lakes Dune Grassland), and Coastal Barrier Resources System designation. This indicator originates from the Great Lakes Aquatic Habitat Framework Harmonized Shoreline Classification, the LANDFIRE Existing Vegetation Type layer, and the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service's Coastal Barrier Resources System designations.

values.label: Not identified as a natural shoreline or dune
Natural shoreline, Coastal Barrier Resources System area, or dune habitat

label: Intact habitat cores
description: This indicator identifies large, unfragmented patches of habitat within the Midwest Landscape. It prioritizes areas based on quality and protection status of habitat cores. This indicator originates from the Esri Green Infrastructure Intact Habitat Cores layer and the Protected Areas Database.

values.label: "Best" intact habitat core score, or GAP 1-2 protected lands

label: Lakes
description: This indicator represents lake health based on watershed condition within the Midwest Landscape. It prioritizes lakes and reservoirs greater than one hectare based on the amount of agriculture and development in a lake's upstream watershed. This indicator originates from the LAGOS-US dataset and Lapierre et al. Continuous Lake Classifications.

values.label: Not identified as a lake or reservoir
Lake or reservoir with high disturbance (>60%) in upstream watershed
Lake or reservoir with medium disturbance (>25-60%) in upstream watershed.
Lake or reservoir with low disturbance (0-25%) in upstream watershed.

label: Landscape condition
description: This indicator represents landscape health based on landdover type and land use across the Midwest Landscape. It prioritizes areas based on proximity to natural or non-natural landcover types, plus intensity of development and land use. This indicator originates from the National Land Cover Database, Maus et al. Global-scale mining polygons, LANDFIRE Existing Vegetation Type, and the Midwest Conservation Blueprint Grasslands indicator.

values.label: Heavily altered landscape or lake (not assessed)

label: Riparian areas
description: This indicator represents riparian health within the Midwest Landscape. It prioritizes areas based on the percentage natural landcover within floodplains. This indicator originates from the EPA Percentage of Land Area in Estimated Floodplains Classified as Natural layer and the EPA Estimated Floodplain Map of the Conterminous U.S.
valueLabel: Percent natural landcover within the estimated floodplain, by catchment

label: Streams
description: This indicator represents stream health based on the risk of habitat degradation within the Midwest Landscape. It prioritizes stream reaches based on urban land use, impervious surface, human population and road density, agriculture, and point source pollution sites. This indicator originates from the National Fish Habitat Partnership Cumulative Habitat Condition Indices scores.

label: Terrestrial habitat connectivity
description: This indicator identifies the ability of terrestrial species to move across the landscape within the Midwest. It prioritizes areas based on relative ease of movement for terrestrial species, factoring in NLCD landcover classes, slope, proximity to water, and habitat core score. This indicator originates from the Esri Green Infrastructure Habitat Cost Surface layer.

label: Wetlands
description: This indicator identifies the availability of wetland habitat within the Midwest Landscape. It prioritizes areas based on whether a pixel can be classified as a wetland. This indicator originates from the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service's National Wetlands Inventory.

label: At-risk species - COAs
description: This indicator identifies the prevalence of species at risk of being threatened with extinction within the Midwest Landscape. It prioritizes areas based on locations of State Conservation Opportunity Areas. Due to the low resolution of the input data, we refine priority areas in this indicator by emphasizing the value of natural landcover types and protected lands, designated as natural assets in the legend. This indicator originates from the State Wildlife Action Plans of the 13 Midwest Association of Fish and Wildlife Agencies (MAFWA) states.

values.label: Not identified as a Conservation Opportunity Area
State Conservation Opportunity Area, not a natural asset
State Conservation Opportunity Area, natural asset

label: At-risk species - RSGCN
description: This indicator identifies the prevalence of species at risk of being threatened with extinction, with an emphasis on at-risk species that are of a regional conservation need. It prioritizes areas based on the rarity-weighted richness of those Regional Species of Greatest Conservation Need (RSGCN) which are not listed as Threatened or Endangered by the ESA or G1 or G2 imperiled species. This indicator originates from the Midwest Regional Species of Greatest Conservation Need database and the USGS Gap Analysis Project.

values.label: Not identified as a priority area for Regional Species of Greatest Conservation Need

label: Game species
description: This indicator identifies the prevalence of and access to socially important game species. It prioritizes areas based on the species richness of 14 commonly hunted species: white-tailed deer, black bear, Rocky Mountain elk, moose, mule deer, pronghorn, wild turkey, bobwhite quail, ring-necked pheasant, American woodcock, ruffed grouse, sharp-tailed grouse, greater prairie chicken, and mourning dove. This indicator originates from the USGS Gap Analysis Project and the National Survey of Fishing, Hunting, and Wildlife-Associated Recreation.

values.label: Not identified as a priority area for game species

label: Imperiled species - critical habitat
description: This indicator identifies the prevalence of species listed as threatened or endangered within the Midwest Landscape. It prioritizes areas based on the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service's final and proposed Critical Habitat designations. Due to the low resolution of the input data, we refine priority areas in this indicator by emphasizing the value of natural landcover types and protected lands, designated as natural assets in the legend. This indicator originates from the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service HQ Critical Habitat layer.

values.label: Critical habitat, not a natural asset

valueLabel: U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service final and proposed critical habitat designations

label: Imperiled species - T&E
description: This indicator identifies the prevalence of species listed as threatened or endangered within the Midwest Landscape. It prioritizes areas based on the rarity-weighted richness of threatened or endangered species found within a 300m pixel. Due to the low resolution of the input data, we refine priority areas in this indicator by emphasizing the value of natural landcover types and protected lands, designated as natural assets in the legend. This indicator originates from the NatureServe Summed Range-size Rarity of Imperiled Species in the United States layer.

values.label: Not identified as a priority area for imperiled species
Low rarity-weighted richness, not a natural asset
Medium rarity-weighted richness, not a natural asset
High rarity-weighted richness, not a natural asset
